name: Refrigerator No-Cheese Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Put the graham crackers in a plastic bag and tie the top. Crush with a rolling pin or jar until fine crumbs are formed.
2. Melt the margarine in the bottom of an 8- or 9-inch round cake pan. Spread evenly over the bottom.
3. Sprinkle the crumbs evenly on the bottom only and press gently.
4. Chill in the refrigerator.
5. Soak the gelatin in cold water, then heat it over boiling water to dissolve.
6. Combine yogurt and the first measure of sugar; beat at moderate speed with rotary beaters, gradually adding the dissolved gelatin.
7. Chill until it reaches the consistency of beaten egg whites.
8. Meanwhile, place iced water, lemon rind, egg white, and dry milk powder in a large bowl. Beat with rotary beaters until soft peaks form.
9. Add lemon juice, vanilla, and the second measure of sugar. Beat at high speed until stiff.
10. Fold into the partially set yogurt; blend very well.
11. Spoon the mixture on top of the crumbs in the pan. Chill for 4 hours or longer until set.
12. To unmold, loosen around the edge of the mold with a thin spatula down to the bottom of the pan.
13. Place a larger plate upside down on top of the mold.
14. Turn the plate and pan over; cover the top of the pan with a hot cloth that has been run under hot water and then rung out.
15. Remove the cloth and lift the pan from the mold.
16. When ready to serve, garnish with cherries or strawberries and cut into six equal slices.
6 Servings 1 Serving: 1/6 Cake
Nutritive values per serving: 13 grams carbohydrates; 8 grams protein; 2 grams fat; 104 calories; 0.3 gram fiber; 124 milligrams sodium; 6 milligrams cholesterol
Food Exchange per serving: 1 Low-Fat Milk Exchange
Low-sodium diets: This recipe is suitable.
